assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, all three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', matching the Security Configuration values (Project key: TC, Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024). The Configuration Used table at the top also explicitly states 'Project key: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024', confirming these were sourced from config rather than hardcoded.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three queries: Query 1 JQL: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged) ORDER BY status ASC, created DESC'; Query 2 JQL: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New ORDER BY created DESC'; Query 3 JQL: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, 'ON_QA') ORDER BY created DESC'. All three match the required patterns.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md has three clearly separated sections with horizontal rules between them: '## Query 1: Untriaged Issues' (lines 15-33), '## Query 2: Triaged but still New' (lines 36-51), and '## Query 3: Ready for QA Candidates' (lines 54-76). Each section is visually and structurally distinct.

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All issues in discovery-listing.md display all five fields. For example, line 27: 'TC-9001 — New — CVE-2026-40112 — h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2] — Created: 2026-06-08' shows issue key (TC-9001), status (New), CVE ID (CVE-2026-40112), summary (h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability), and created date (2026-06-08). The same pattern holds for all issues: TC-9002, TC-9003, TC-9004, TC-9010, TC-9020, TC-9023, TC-9026.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001 (line 21): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.'; TC-9002 (line 26): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.'; TC-9004 (line 44): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.'; TC-9003 (lines 30-39): 'Handling: Warn the user before proceeding: "This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on."' with options to proceed or skip.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: TC-9020 is listed under 'Ready for QA (1 issue)' with 'Remediation Tasks: TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)' (line 66-68). TC-9023 is under 'Excluded from Ready for QA' with reason 'Remediation in progress: TC-9025 still In Progress (TC-9024 Done)' (line 74). TC-9026 is under 'Excluded from Ready for QA' with reason 'No linked Tasks with type "Depend" — no remediation to verify' (line 75).
